zh.lifeSummary: 當Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg於1224年出生在賀爾左圖姆 薩辛-可包格 武德 格塔可包格時，他的父親Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg41歲，母親Jutta von Thüringen11221歲。他於從1249年1月8日到1250年1月7日與Margaretha van Holland結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1290年12月18日在賀爾左圖姆 薩辛-可包格 武德 格塔史馬爾卡爾登享年66歲，埋葬在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 巴德 吉辛根。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg于1224年出生在賀爾左圖姆 薩辛-可包格 武德 格塔可包格时，他的父亲Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg41岁，母亲Jutta von Thüringen11221岁。他于从1249年1月8日到1250年1月7日与Margaretha van Holland结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1290年12月18日在賀爾左圖姆 薩辛-可包格 武德 格塔史馬爾卡爾登享年66岁，埋葬在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 巴德 吉辛根。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Henneberg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Beate, Ewald, Kurt, Ulrich.
German: habitational name from any of several places so named.

variants.name: Henneberger
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Fritz, Gerhard.
German: habitational name for someone from a place called Henneberg (see   Henneberg  ).

variants.name: Henneberry
meanings: Americanized form of German and Dutch   Henneberg  .

nameMeanings.name: Hermann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heri ‘army’ + man ‘man’.

en.lifeSummary: When Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg was born in 1224, in Coburg, Saxe-Coburg and Gotha, his father, Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg, was 41 and his mother, Jutta von Thüringen, was 11221. He married Margaretha van Holland from 8 January 1249 to 7 January 1250. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 December 1290, in Schmalkalden, Saxe-Coburg and Gotha, at the age of 66, and was buried in Bad Kissingen, Bavaria, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg est né en 1224, à Coburg, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, son père, Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Jutta von Thüringen, avait 11221 ans. Il a épousé Margaretha van Holland du 8 janvier 1249 au 7 janvier 1250. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 décembre 1290, à Schmalkalden, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Landkreis Bad Kissingen, Bayern, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg 1224 in Coburg, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha geboren wurde, war sein Vater Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg 41 und seine Mutter Jutta von Thüringen 11221. Er hat von 8. Januar 1249 bis 7. Januar 1250 Margaretha van Holland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 18. Dezember 1290 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Schmalkalden, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha und wurde in Landkreis Bad Kissingen, Bayern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg è nato il nel 1224 nel Coburg, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, il suo padre, Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Jutta von Thüringen, aveva 11221 anni. Lui sposato Margaretha van Holland da 8 gennaio 1249 a 7 gennaio 1250. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 18 dicembre 1290, nel Schmalkalden, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Landkreis Bad Kissingen, Bayern, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Hermann I. Graf von Hennebergが1224年にハーゾグトゥム サチセン-コブルグ ウンド ゴサコブルグで生まれた時，父Graf Poppo VII von Hennebergは41歳，母Jutta von Thüringenは11221歳であった。から1249年1月8日まで1250年1月7日にMargaretha van Hollandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1290年12月18日にハーゾグトゥム サチセン-コブルグ ウンド ゴサスチマルカルデンで66歳で死去し，ドイツバイエルンランドクレイス バド キシンジェンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg(이)가 1224년 허초그투 작센-컵르그 운드 가스아 컵르그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Jutta von Thüringen(은)는 11221세였다. 그는 1249년 1월 8일부터 1250년 1월 7일까지 Margaretha van Holland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1290년 12월 18일 허초그투 작센-컵르그 운드 가스아 슈마캘든에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 바드 키징에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg nasceu em 1224, em Coburg, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, seu pai, Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Jutta von Thüringen, tinha 11221 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaretha van Holland de 8 de janeiro de 1249 a 7 de janeiro de 1250. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 18 de dezembro de 1290, em Schmalkalden, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Landkreis Bad Kissingen, Bayern, Alemanha.

ru.Aschach, Unterfranken, Bavaria, Germany: ащач, бад бокклет, ландкрис бад киссинген, беиирн, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg родился в 1224 г. в местности кобург, хирзогтум сачсен-кобург унд гота, его отцу по имени Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Jutta von Thüringen, 11221. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaretha van Holland с 8 января 1249 по 7 января 1250. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 декабря 1290 г. в местности щмалкалден, хирзогтум сачсен-кобург унд гота в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности ландкрис бад киссинген, беиирн, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hermann I. Graf von Henneberg nació en 1224, en Coburg, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, su papá, Graf Poppo VII von Henneberg, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Jutta von Thüringen, tenía 11221 años. Se casó con Margaretha van Holland desde 8 de enero de 1249 hasta 7 de enero de 1250. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de diciembre de 1290, en Schmalkalden, Herzogtum Sachsen-Coburg und Gotha, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Landkreis Bad Kissingen, Bayern, Alemania.
